name: trivy-cluster-scan
description: Authorized, scan-only Trivy security assessment for Kubernetes or service clusters, container images, image configuration, Kubernetes resource configuration, secrets, misconfigurations, vulnerabilities, optional runtime/node findings, and official remediation guidance.
capabilities: download portable Trivy from official Aqua Security GitHub releases
verify Trivy archive SHA256 against release checksums
scan Kubernetes clusters with trivy k8s
scan container images with trivy image
scan application source paths with trivy fs
deduplicate vulnerability findings for remediation research
produce scan-only reports with official remediation citations

runtime_requirements.network: Required by default to download portable Trivy and verify latest official remediation guidance. Use --trivy-path for offline Trivy.
runtime_requirements.cluster_access: Requires an authorized kubeconfig, context, kubectl access, or authorized remote execution path supplied by the user.
runtime_requirements.trivy: Downloaded ephemerally by the bundled script unless --trivy-path is supplied.

safety_boundaries: Use Trivy as the only vulnerability scanning engine.
Run only against user-owned or explicitly authorized targets.
Do not fix, patch, apply, delete, upgrade, restart, scale, mutate manifests, open remediation PRs, or run exploit payloads.
Provide remediation recommendations as text only unless a separate human-confirmed remediation task is requested.
Runtime/node collector may create and delete temporary Kubernetes scan jobs and requires explicit user confirmation.
Redact secrets and do not print full secret values.

install_hint.generic_agent: Read skill.json, then load SKILL.md as the primary instruction file. Keep scripts/ and references/ at the same relative paths.
install_hint.codex_like: Create a skill directory named trivy-cluster-scan under the agent skills directory and copy all files from this package into it.
install_hint.root_zip: This archive is intentionally flat: SKILL.md is at the ZIP root for agents that only inspect top-level files.
